summary: Matched __OSInitSystemCall in src/dolphin/os/OSSync.c by replacing the NON_MATCHING C/handwritten asm fallback with a compact C implementation. The matching version stores OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER in a local handler variable, copies the system call vector by subtracting the start/end function addresses as u32 values, flushes data cache, executes __sync, and invalidates instruction cache. The PR also removed unnecessary #pragma push/pop around the nearby asm vector wrapper.
what_changed: Removed the #ifdef NON_MATCHING split for __OSInitSystemCall and deleted the handwritten asm implementation.
Kept __OSInitSystemCall as C and adjusted it to match the original code generation.
Introduced a local void* handler initialized to OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER, then reused it for memcpy, DCFlushRangeNoSync, and ICInvalidateRange.
Changed the memcpy length expression to cast __OSSystemCallVectorStart and __OSSystemCallVectorEnd to u32 before subtracting.
Preserved the cache-management sequence: memcpy to OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER, DCFlushRangeNoSync, __sync, then ICInvalidateRange.
smart_moves: Using a local handler variable likely encouraged the compiler to keep the system call handler address in a register and reuse it across calls, matching the deleted asm's use of r31.
Casting vector start/end symbols to u32 before subtracting made the size calculation more closely resemble the target PowerPC integer address arithmetic.
The code retains the exact ordering of memory copy, data-cache flush, sync, and instruction-cache invalidate, which is important for a low-level OS vector install routine.
The explicit comment, "peephole off is needed to match," documents a compiler-codegen dependency for future maintainers.
decomp_lessons: For low-level init functions that repeatedly use a fixed hardware address, introducing a local pointer can be a matching tactic because it may force register preservation/reuse similar to original asm.
When computing a code/vector length from linker symbols, integer casts on addresses may be necessary to produce the desired subtraction codegen.
Do not assume a simple pointer-difference expression will match; the original code may be better modeled as raw address arithmetic.
When replacing asm with C, preserve side-effect ordering around cache and sync operations exactly.
If a match depends on compiler peephole optimization settings, leave an inline note near the function.
naming_conventions: Dolphin OS internal functions retain double-underscore names such as __OSInitSystemCall, __OSSystemCallVectorStart, and __OSSystemCallVectorEnd.
Hardware/system constants use all-caps names such as OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER and OS_HANDLER_SLOT_SIZE.
Cache helper names preserve Dolphin SDK-style casing: ICInvalidateRange and DCFlushRangeNoSync.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a full asm implementation once the C version matched.
Used a local variable to model repeated use of OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER and likely match callee-saved register behavior.
Used u32 casts on symbol addresses for the vector size calculation.
The diff notes that peephole optimization must be disabled for this function to match.
Removed NON_MATCHING guard after achieving a C match.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that mention peephole off for matching.
Find other Dolphin OS functions that compute sizes from linker symbol start/end labels.
Find other matches where a local pointer variable was used to force register reuse.
Find remaining NON_MATCHING blocks in src/dolphin/os.
